category_title: Students with SEBD (ADHD/ADD) who struggle with passive learning and traditional instruction
scenario_title: Passive whiteboard instruction during abstract trigonometry lesson
minimal_context: The student is sitting alone in a mainstream mathematics class, tasked with copying trigonometry formulas from the whiteboard.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please sit down at your desk and copy the trigonometry formulas from the whiteboard.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I feel bored all the time sitting down. Why do we have to copy all the time from the whiteboard and gaze at it?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Just focus on the board and write down what you see.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Mentally I cannot understand this. I can't just sit here and be like a statue.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You need to work alone and finish copying the notes.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I prefer standing up and working in groups rather than alone! (Stands up abruptly and starts fighting with the student at the next desk)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please sit down at your desk and copy the trigonometry formulas from the whiteboard.

STUDENT: I feel bored all the time sitting down. Why do we have to copy all the time from the whiteboard and gaze at it?

TUTOR: Just focus on the board and write down what you see.

STUDENT: Mentally I cannot understand this. I can't just sit here and be like a statue.

TUTOR: You need to work alone and finish copying the notes.

STUDENT: I prefer standing up and working in groups rather than alone! (Stands up abruptly and starts fighting with the student at the next desk)

task.content: Discussing trigonometry and being expected to copy all the time from the whiteboard and gaze at it

context.environment: Mainstream mathematics class, with access to school grounds

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When expected to be like statues, sit down in class and see things on the whiteboard, and understand concepts mentally without visual aids
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Fights with the other students and sometimes even with teachers
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Feels bored all the time sitting down; mentally cannot understand
related_quotes: Here [i.e., core programme mathematics class] I never fight with the other students or with the teacher...but in the other class [i.e., mainstream] I fight with the other students and sometimes even with teachers.
In the other class [i.e., mainstream] I feel bored all the time sitting down.
Mentally I cannot understand, but visually I can cope...pictures make a big difference for me...I wouldn’t have understood without them.

behavior_patterns.condition: When required to sit still and work alone on abstract concepts
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Exhibits disruptive behaviour and fights with peers or teachers
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Boredom and inability to cope mentally
related_quotes: I prefer standing up and working in groups rather than alone.
Mentally I cannot understand, but visually I can cope
